items.title: xAI launched Grok 4.5 with Cursor
items.oneliner: Grok 4.5 claims Opus-class coding at $2/$6 per million tokens. Capability-per-dollar is now the real leaderboard.

items.title: OpenAI shipped full-duplex GPT-Live voice
items.oneliner: OpenAI says users preferred GPT-Live in 75% of tests. Real-time interruption makes voice agents finally feel native.

items.title: Cognition released a cheaper SWE-1.7
items.oneliner: Cognition says SWE-1.7 approaches GPT-5.5 and Opus-level coding quality at lower cost. Budget coding agents just got credible.

items.title: ByteDance pushed image models toward design
items.oneliner: Seedream 5.0 Pro adds multilingual editing and design-aware generation. Image models are moving from art toys to workflows.

items.title: The EU advanced chat scanning powers
items.oneliner: The EU greenlit interim chat scanning through 2028. Privacy-preserving product claims now face a much harder regulatory reality.

items.title: OpenBSD disclosed a local root bug
items.oneliner: OpenBSD through 7.9 has a CVSS 7.4 use-after-free that enables local root escalation. Hardened systems still need patch discipline.

items.title: OpenMandriva says a contributor sabotaged repos
items.oneliner: OpenMandriva says a former contributor deleted repos and pushed an empty package. Open source governance is now supply-chain security.

items.title: Microsoft announced a much faster TypeScript
items.oneliner: TypeScript 7.0 moves core tooling to a native Go port and claims 10x speedups. Frontend build latency is finally attackable.

items.title: Meta is reusing old RAM at scale
items.oneliner: Meta built a custom CXL bridge to reuse retired DIMMs in new servers. Memory scarcity is forcing hardware creativity.

items.title: Databricks benchmarked coding agents on production code
items.oneliner: Databricks found model price poorly predicts coding performance on a multi-million-line codebase. Eval harnesses now matter as much as models.
